title: mentha arvensis var. longibracteata
intro: Mentha arvensis var. longibracteata, also known as Japanese wild mint, is a subspecies of the wild mint plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its essential oils, including menthone and limonene. The medicinal properties of Japanese wild mint are believed to offer various health benefits, including relieving digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and exhibiting antiseptic properties to aid in wound healing. This plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
